# QA Standards — Generated Content Validation

**Purpose:** Every piece of content generated by the AI-SIEM Hyperautomation application (detections, dashboards, workflows, parsers) must pass these checks before it is presented to the user for approval and before any PR is submitted to `Sentinel-One/ai-siem`.

**Source of truth for patterns:** `ai-siem/` (clone) and `reference/APPROVED_CONTENT_WHITELIST.md`

---

## Validation Tiers

| Tier | Gate | Who runs it |
|---|---|---|
| T1 | Structure & schema validation | Application (automated) |
| T2 | Pattern match vs approved whitelist | Application (automated) |
| T3 | Functional review | User approval (human-in-loop) |
| T4 | Pre-PR checklist | Application + user |

Nothing advances from T3 → T4 without explicit user approval.

---

## T1 — Structure & Schema Validation

### Required Files per Content Type

Every piece of content **must** have both a definition file AND a `metadata.yaml`.

| Content Type | Definition File | Metadata File |
|---|---|---|
| Detection | `<name>.conf` (PowerQuery) | `metadata.yaml` |
| Dashboard | `<name>.conf` or `<name>.json` | `metadata.yaml` |
| Workflow | `<name>.json` | `metadata.yaml` |
| Parser | `<name>.conf` | `metadata.yaml` |
| Pipeline | `<name>.json` | `metadata.yaml` |

### Required `metadata.yaml` Fields by Type

**Detections:**
```yaml
purpose: "..."
mitre_tactic_technique: "Tactic > Technique > Sub-technique"
datasource: "exact dataSource.name value"
search_type: "powerquery | star_rule | watchlist_alert"
usecase_plus: "..."
severity: "Information | Low | Medium | High"
expected_alert_scenario: "..."
performance_impact: "..."
tags: []
version: "v1.0"
```

**Dashboards:**
```yaml
data_dependencies: "datasource.name or OCSF field"
required_fields: "..."
description: "..."
usecase_type: "Operational | Security | Compliance"
usecase_action: "Formfill | Dashboard | Report | Trending and Analysis"
tags: []
version: "v1.0"
```

**Workflows:**
```yaml
purpose: "..."
trigger_type: "alert | manual | scheduled | webhook"
integration_dependency: "..."
expected_actions_per_run: N
human_in_the_loop: "yes | no"
required_products: "AI SIEM, HyperAutomation [, ...]"
tags: []
version: "v1.0"
```

**Parsers:**
```yaml
purpose: "..."
datasource_vendor: "..."
dataSource: "exact dataSource.name value"
format: "gron | json | xml | raw | syslog"
ingestion_method: "streaming | syslog | HEC | Agent Ingest"
sample_record: "..."
dependency_summary: "..."
performance_impact: "..."
tags: []
version: "v1.0"
```

**Pipelines:**
```yaml
purpose: "..."
datasource_vendor: "..."
ingestion_method: "..."
required_credentials: []
output: "SentinelOne AI SIEM HEC"
tags: []
version: "v1.0"
```

### Naming Conventions

- **Directory:** `<vendor>_<product>-latest` or `<vendor>_<product>-v<X.Y>`
- **Definition file:** matches directory name + extension
- **No spaces in directory names** (workflows are the exception — upstream uses spaces)
- **Lowercase snake_case** for vendor/product names

### Security Checks (auto-blocked)

- No hardcoded API keys, tokens, passwords, or credentials anywhere in content
- No real HEC tokens (format: 40–50 alphanumeric chars)
- No real IP addresses from private ranges in query logic unless templated
- No `.env` or credential file references

---

## T2 — Pattern Match vs Approved Whitelist

### Detection Validation Checklist

- [ ] Query language is **PowerQuery** (all approved detections use PowerQuery)
- [ ] `dataSource.name` or `dataSource.vendor` value matches a known approved value from the whitelist
- [ ] MITRE ATT&CK tactic and technique are valid (check against ATT&CK framework)
- [ ] Severity is one of: `Information`, `Low`, `Medium`, `High`
- [ ] Alert threshold logic is consistent with approved patterns:
  - Volume-based: use rolling time windows (e.g., 4 min, 8 hours, 1 hour, 30 min)
  - Behavioral: compare against baseline (e.g., user-per-day averages)
  - Single-event: simple field match rules
- [ ] If response action is a webhook, destination must be parameterized (no hardcoded URLs)

### Dashboard Validation Checklist

- [ ] `data_dependencies` references a data source present in the approved parser list or whitelist
- [ ] `usecase_type` is one of: `Operational`, `Security`, `Compliance`
- [ ] `.conf` files use valid PowerQuery syntax (cross-check field names against approved parsers)
- [ ] `.json` files (new format) are valid JSON parseable without errors
- [ ] Dashboard name does not duplicate an existing approved entry

### Workflow Validation Checklist

- [ ] `trigger_type` is one of the approved values: `alert`, `manual`, `scheduled`, `webhook`, `automatic`
- [ ] All referenced integrations have documented API requirements in metadata
- [ ] `human_in_the_loop: yes` is set for any workflow with destructive or irreversible actions:
  - User disablement
  - Password reset
  - Firewall rule changes
  - Data deletion
- [ ] `required_products` lists only valid SentinelOne product names
- [ ] Workflow JSON is structurally valid (parseable)
- [ ] No API credentials hardcoded in workflow JSON
- [ ] If the workflow pattern matches an existing approved workflow, generate a comparison diff

### Parser Validation Checklist

- [ ] `dataSource` value follows the approved naming pattern from the whitelist
- [ ] `format` is one of: `gron`, `json`, `xml`, `raw`, `syslog`
- [ ] `ingestion_method` is one of: `streaming`, `syslog`, `HEC`, `Agent Ingest`
- [ ] `sample_record` field is populated and realistic (no PII)
- [ ] Parser does not duplicate an existing approved entry (check against full whitelist)

### Pipeline Validation Checklist

- [ ] Pipeline JSON is valid and parseable
- [ ] All credential fields use placeholder values (not real credentials)
- [ ] Output endpoint is parameterized (not hardcoded to a specific region/tenant)
- [ ] Matches the Observo pipeline template format used by approved pipelines

---

## T3 — User Approval Gate

Before any generated content is finalized:

1. Present a **structured diff** against the most similar approved content from the whitelist
2. Show the full `metadata.yaml` for review
3. Show the definition file (detection query, dashboard config, workflow JSON)
4. Explicitly state:
   - What new capability this adds that doesn't exist in the approved whitelist
   - Any areas of uncertainty or assumptions made
5. User must explicitly approve before T4 proceeds

**Format for user review:**

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ GENERATED CONTENT REVIEW                                     │
│ Type: [Detection | Dashboard | Workflow | Parser | Pipeline] │
│ Name: [name]                                                 │
│ Closest approved baseline: [whitelist entry]                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

[metadata.yaml]
[definition file preview]

GAPS vs BASELINE:
- [differences from closest approved pattern]

ASSUMPTIONS:
- [any assumptions made in generation]

→ Type "approve" to proceed to PR prep, or describe changes needed.
```

---

## T4 — Pre-PR Checklist

Before submitting a PR to `Sentinel-One/ai-siem`:

- [ ] T1 structure validation passed
- [ ] T2 pattern validation passed
- [ ] T3 user approval received (timestamp recorded)
- [ ] Content placed in correct directory structure matching upstream repo layout
- [ ] Branch name follows pattern: `feat/<vendor>-<usecase>-<type>` (e.g., `feat/cloudflare-waf-detection`)
- [ ] PR title ≤ 70 characters
- [ ] PR description includes: what it detects/does, data source required, tested against sample events
- [ ] No secrets in diff (TruffleHog will auto-scan on PR — pre-check before submitting)
- [ ] `metadata.yaml` complete with all required fields
- [ ] At least one sample log/event included in PR description or `tests/fixtures/`

See `reference/PR_GUIDE.md` for the exact PR submission commands.

---

## Known-Good Field Names (from approved content)

Use these exact field names when generating detection queries — they are confirmed to work:

**Identity/Auth:**
- `user.name`, `user.username`, `user.email`
- `auth_requirement`, `status_detail`
- `event.details`

**Network:**
- `src_ip`, `dst_ip`, `src_port`, `dst_port`
- `activity_name`
- `dlpengine`

**Data Source:**
- `dataSource.name`, `dataSource.vendor`, `dataSource.product`

**Volume:**
- `logVolume` (tag used for log volume monitoring dashboards)

**Geolocation:**
- `geo_distance()`, `geo_ip_location()` (PowerQuery functions confirmed working)

**Time:**
- Standard PowerQuery time windows: `4m`, `8h`, `1h`, `30m`, `1d`
